title: Coral Reef Survey: Robust Transect Balancing
strategies: reference reproduction: emit the constant, conservative extragradient step (alpha_k = beta_k = 0.4/||H||) the evaluator uses as its weak baseline -> ~0.1 everywhere
edge-of-stability constant step: double the reference step to 0.8/||H|| so the fixed-step method contracts low-frequency error faster, but with no spectral adaptation it plateaus

Chebyshev semi-iterative schedule: read H, take the real-part spectral interval [a,b], and set the descent-ascent steps to reciprocals of the Chebyshev nodes on [a,b] for optimal min-residual acceleration

spectrum-aware / extragradient designs: use the full extragradient extrapolation (alpha_k>0) or momentum/optimistic corrections to damp the rotational (imaginary) part of the saddle field beyond a real-interval Chebyshev design
